customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety. Nick is a warm inbound referral from his father-in-law (Leland Fan, active Ion customer at 21121 East Jefferson Avenue, Aurora CO), self-motivated, cooperative, and already treating this as a household decision. No skepticism signals on the call.
unresolved_concerns: Community solar arrangement through Xcel Energy — Nick is enrolled in a community solar subscription that offsets a portion of his electricity. The closer needs to understand how this affects his current bill, his net usage, and how rooftop solar interacts with the community solar contract. This was acknowledged but never probed on the setter call.
Average monthly bill unknown — the rep never asked. The closer will need to establish this at the start of the appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Community solar subscription through Xcel — Nick raised this as a potential complication and was not given a clear answer. He may arrive at the appointment still uncertain whether it affects his eligibility or system design.
exact_promises_rep_made: Saturday the 18th at 2PM appointment with a local specialist
One hour blocked off so Nick and his wife don't feel rushed
Confirmation text or email will be sent once the bill is received
closer_should_know: The confirmation text has NOT been sent yet — it is gated on Nick emailing his Xcel bill, which he said he'd do when he has time (he was at work during the call). If the bill hasn't arrived by Friday, the appointment has no confirmation anchor. Closer or floor coordinator should verify the bill was received and the confirmation was sent before Saturday. Additionally, Nick has a community solar arrangement through Xcel that the rep acknowledged but did not probe — the closer should ask about it early to understand how it affects the bill and the design.
